title: Critical Package Coverage - auth-ui, billing, logger, email

tasks.title: Install @testing-library/user-event in auth-ui
tasks.description: Run `cd packages/auth-ui && pnpm add -D @testing-library/user-event`. Verify it appears in package.json devDependencies. Run `pnpm install` from root to update lockfile.

tasks.title: Install @react-email/render in email package
tasks.description: Run `cd packages/email && pnpm add -D @react-email/render`. Verify it appears in package.json devDependencies. Run `pnpm install` from root to update lockfile.

tasks.title: Export shouldUseWhiteText and redactSensitiveData from logger formatter
tasks.description: In `packages/logger/src/formatter.ts`, add `export` keyword to `function redactSensitiveData` (~line 130) and `function shouldUseWhiteText` (~line 239). Then add re-exports in `packages/logger/src/index.ts` if not already re-exported. This is the ONLY source code modification allowed in the entire spec. Verify with `pnpm typecheck` that no type errors are introduced.

subtasks.title: Add export to redactSensitiveData in formatter.ts
Add export to shouldUseWhiteText in formatter.ts
Add re-exports in index.ts if needed
Run pnpm typecheck on logger package

tasks.title: Create vitest.config.ts for email package
tasks.description: Create `packages/email/vitest.config.ts` with environment: 'node', include: ['test/**/*.test.ts', 'test/**/*.test.tsx'], coverage provider v8, thresholds at 90% (lines, functions, branches, statements), include: ['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx'], exclude test files. Use packages/auth-ui/vitest.config.ts as reference. Template is provided in spec section 'packages/email test strategy'.

subtasks.title: Verify config is valid by running pnpm vitest --config packages/email/vitest.config.ts --passWithNoTests

tasks.title: Remove basic.test.tsx and write SignInForm render + hydration tests
tasks.description: Delete `packages/auth-ui/test/basic.test.tsx` (it tests fake components). Create `packages/auth-ui/test/sign-in-form.test.tsx`. Test scenarios: (1) shows skeleton/loading before isClientReady, (2) after hydration renders email + password fields, (3) shows OAuth buttons when showOAuth=true (default), (4) hides OAuth buttons when showOAuth=false. Mock `@repo/i18n` with `vi.mock('@repo/i18n', () => ({ useTranslations: () => (key: string) => key }))`. Use `waitFor` for hydration. Import types from `../src/types`.

subtasks.title: Create sign-in-form.test.tsx with imports and mock setup
Test: renders email + password after hydration
Test: OAuth buttons shown by default
Test: OAuth buttons hidden with showOAuth=false

tasks.title: Write SignInForm submit, error handling, and redirect tests
tasks.description: Add tests to `packages/auth-ui/test/sign-in-form.test.tsx`. Scenarios: (1) calls signIn.email({email, password}) on submit, (2) shows loading state during submit, (3) shows error when result.error is present, (4) calls onSuccess on successful login, (5) redirects via window.location.replace(redirectTo) on success, (6) does NOT redirect when redirectTo is undefined, (7) does not crash when onSuccess is undefined, (8) handles signIn.email throwing exception gracefully (try/catch path, logs via authLogger). Use userEvent.setup() for form interactions. Mock window.location with `{ replace: vi.fn(), href: '', pathname: '/' }`.

subtasks.title: Test: calls signIn.email with correct params
Test: shows error message from result.error
Test: graceful handling of thrown exception

tasks.title: Write SignInForm OAuth tests
tasks.description: Add tests to `packages/auth-ui/test/sign-in-form.test.tsx`. Scenarios: (1) calls signIn.social({ provider: 'google', callbackURL }) on Google button click, (2) calls signIn.social({ provider: 'facebook', callbackURL }) on Facebook button click, (3) callbackURL is calculated as `redirectTo ?? window.location.pathname ?? '/'`. Verify exact params passed to signIn.social.

subtasks.title: Test: Google OAuth calls signIn.social correctly
Test: Facebook OAuth calls signIn.social correctly

tasks.title: Write SignUpForm tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/auth-ui/test/sign-up-form.test.tsx`. Scenarios: (1) skeleton before hydration, (2) renders name, email, password fields after hydration, (3) calls signUp.email({name, email, password}) on submit, (4) shows error on failure, (5) loading state during submit, (6) OAuth buttons shown/hidden, (7) calls signIn.social for Google/Facebook (uses signIn prop, not signUp), (8) calls onSuccess on success, (9) redirects via window.location.replace(redirectTo), (10) graceful handling of signUp.email exception. Same mock patterns as SignInForm tests. IMPORTANT: SignUpForm needs BOTH signUp AND signIn props.

tasks.title: Write ForgotPasswordForm tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/auth-ui/test/forgot-password-form.test.tsx`. ALL text is hardcoded ENGLISH. Scenarios: (1) renders email field with placeholder 'you@example.com', (2) renders heading 'Reset your password', (3) renders 'Back to sign in' link using signInUrl prop (default '/auth/signin'), (4) calls onForgotPassword({email, redirectTo}) on submit with redirectTo default '/auth/reset-password', (5) shows 'Check your email' + success message on success, (6) shows error from result.error.message, (7) loading state shows 'Sending...' instead of 'Send reset link', (8) validates empty email shows 'Please enter your email address', (9) graceful handling of thrown exception shows 'An unexpected error...' (NO authLogger). No i18n mock needed.

tasks.title: Write ResetPasswordForm tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/auth-ui/test/reset-password-form.test.tsx`. ALL text hardcoded ENGLISH. Token is a PROP (do NOT mock URLSearchParams). Scenarios: (1) renders heading 'Set new password', (2) renders password fields with placeholders 'At least 8 characters' / 'Repeat your password', (3) validates passwords match ('Passwords do not match'), (4) validates min length ('Password must be at least 8 characters'), (5) no token shows 'Invalid or missing reset token' + no form, (6) calls onResetPassword({newPassword, token}) on submit, (7) success shows 'Password reset successful' + message + 'Sign in' link, (8) error from result.error.message, (9) loading 'Resetting...' instead of 'Reset password', (10) calls onSuccess, (11) signInUrl prop renders link, (12) exception handling shows 'An unexpected error...' (NO authLogger).

tasks.title: Write VerifyEmail tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/auth-ui/test/verify-email.test.tsx`. ALL text hardcoded ENGLISH. Token is PROP. Scenarios: (1) no token shows 'Invalid or missing verification token' + no verification attempt, (2) calls onVerifyEmail({token}) automatically on mount, (3) loading state 'Verifying your email...' + 'Please wait...', (4) success 'Email verified' + 'Your email has been verified successfully.' + appends ' Redirecting...' if redirectDelay>0, (5) shows 'Continue' button in success, (6) error with result.error.message: heading 'Verification failed', body shows error.message, (7) error without message: body shows 'Verification failed', (8) exception: body shows 'An unexpected error occurred during verification.', (9) all errors show 'Please try signing in again...' text, (10) calls onSuccess, (11) redirects to redirectTo (default '/') via window.location.href after redirectDelay ms, (12) no redirect when redirectDelay=0. Use vi.useFakeTimers for redirect delay.

subtasks.title: Test: no token error + auto-call on mount
Test: 3 error scenarios (with msg, without msg, exception)

tasks.title: Write SignOutButton tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/auth-ui/test/sign-out-button.test.tsx`. Text hardcoded SPANISH. Scenarios: (1) returns null when isAuthenticated=false, (2) renders button when isAuthenticated=true, (3) button text is 'Cerrar sesion' (hardcoded Spanish, no accent), (4) calls onSignOut on click, (5) calls onComplete after onSignOut resolves, (6) navigates to redirectTo via window.location.href after sign out, (7) handles onSignOut error gracefully (logs via authLogger, no crash), (8) accepts className and applies to button, (9) default style includes red background.

tasks.title: Write UserMenu tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/auth-ui/test/user-menu.test.tsx`. Mixed hardcoded text: 'Dashboard' (EN), 'Mi Perfil' (ES), 'Cerrar Sesion' (ES). Scenarios: (1) loading skeleton when isPending=true, (2) returns null when session is null, (3) shows avatar with initial when no image, (4) shows user image when session.user.image exists, (5) displayName fallback: user.name -> user.email -> 'User', (6) dropdown opens on avatar click, (7) dropdown closes on backdrop click, (8) dropdown closes on Escape, (9) dropdown has Dashboard (dashboardUrl default '/dashboard/') and Mi Perfil (profileUrl default '/profile/') links, (10) 'Cerrar Sesion' calls onSignOut, (11) after sign out navigates to '/' via window.location.href ONLY if !pathname.includes('/auth'), (12) does NOT redirect if on /auth/* page, (13) aria-expanded reflects dropdown state, (14) aria-haspopup='true' on trigger.

tasks.title: Write SimpleUserMenu tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/auth-ui/test/simple-user-menu.test.tsx`. Hardcoded SPANISH: 'Iniciar sesion', 'Registrarse', 'Cerrar sesion'. Scenarios: (1) loading skeleton when isPending=true, (2) shows sign-in + sign-up links when session is null, (3) sign-up button has gradient background, (4) sign-in URL: '/auth/signin/' (trailing slash), (5) sign-up URL: '/auth/signup/' (trailing slash), (6) shows user info (avatar, name, email) when authenticated, (7) uses session.user.image for avatar if available, gradient+initial if not, (8) 'Cerrar sesion' calls onSignOut, (9) navigates to redirectTo (default '/') after sign out, (10) handles onSignOut error gracefully.

subtasks.title: Test: sign out + redirect + error handling

tasks.title: Write useAuthTranslations hook tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/auth-ui/test/hooks/use-auth-translations.test.ts`. Scenarios: (1) with @repo/i18n mocked to work: returns {t, isI18nAvailable: true} and t() returns translations, (2) with @repo/i18n mocked to throw: returns {t: getFallbackText, isI18nAvailable: false}, (3) fallback returns Spanish text for known keys, (4) fallback returns the key itself for unknown keys, (5) parameter replacement: t('signUp.passwordMinLength', {min: 8}) replaces {min} correctly, (6) DOCUMENT the inconsistency: keys used by SignUpForm (auth-ui.signUp.name, namePlaceholder, emailPlaceholder, passwordPlaceholder) do NOT have fallbacks in the map. Use renderHook from @testing-library/react.

tasks.title: Write limits.config.ts tests for billing
tasks.description: Create `packages/billing/test/limits.test.ts`. Import with .js extension: `import { LIMIT_METADATA } from '../src/config/limits.config.js'`. Scenarios: (1) LIMIT_METADATA has entries for all 6 LimitKeys: MAX_ACCOMMODATIONS, MAX_PHOTOS_PER_ACCOMMODATION, MAX_ACTIVE_PROMOTIONS, MAX_FAVORITES, MAX_PROPERTIES, MAX_STAFF_ACCOUNTS, (2) every entry has non-empty name string, (3) every entry has non-empty description string, (4) no duplicate keys, (5) keys match LimitKey enum values exactly.

tasks.title: Add entitlements.config cross-validation tests
tasks.description: Add tests to `packages/billing/test/entitlements.test.ts` (or create new file if cleaner). Import with .js extension. Scenarios: (1) all EntitlementKey enum values have a definition in ENTITLEMENT_DEFINITIONS, (2) no duplicate keys in ENTITLEMENT_DEFINITIONS, (3) all categories exist (owner, accommodation, complex, tourist), (4) category counts: 12 owner, 7 accommodation, 6 complex, 15 tourist. Follow existing test patterns in the 9 existing test files.

subtasks.title: Test: all enum keys have definitions

tasks.title: Add config-validator edge case tests for billing
tasks.description: Add edge case tests to `packages/billing/test/validation/config-validator.test.ts`. Review existing 36 test cases and add any missing branches from the source code. Potential gaps: (1) validateBillingConfigOrThrow throws when errors exist, (2) validateBillingConfigOrThrow does not throw when config is valid, (3) any uncovered branch in the validation logic. Follow existing patterns in the file (621 lines). Use .js imports.

subtasks.title: Review existing 36 test cases against source branches

tasks.title: Write formatter.ts formatValue tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/logger/test/formatter.test.ts`. Test `formatValue` function. Scenarios: (1) null -> 'null', (2) undefined -> 'undefined', (3) short string returns unchanged, (4) long string with truncateText=true truncates with '...[TRUNCATED]' (no space), (5) long string with truncateText=false does not truncate, (6) object with expandLevels=2 returns expanded JSON, (7) object with expandLevels=0 returns '[Object]', (8) object with expandLevels=-1 returns full JSON, (9) array of objects expanded correctly, (10) circular reference contains '[Circular]', (11) object with 'password' key -> value '[REDACTED]', (12) email in string -> '[REDACTED]', (13) JWT in string -> '[REDACTED]', (14) Bearer token -> '[REDACTED]', (15) credit card number -> '[REDACTED]', (16) IPv4 -> '[REDACTED]', (17) CUIT/CUIL -> '[REDACTED]', (18) Argentine phone -> '[REDACTED]', (19) API key with sk_ prefix -> '[REDACTED]', (20) non-sensitive data not redacted.

tasks.title: Write formatter.ts formatLogMessage and formatLogArgs tests
tasks.description: Add to `packages/logger/test/formatter.test.ts`. Test `formatLogMessage`: (1) without category no category prefix, (2) with registered category includes name in uppercase, (3) with INCLUDE_TIMESTAMPS=true includes [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS], (4) with INCLUDE_LEVEL=true includes [INFO]/[ERROR] etc, (5) with label includes [label], (6) ERROR level with TRUNCATE_LONG_TEXT_ON_ERROR=false doesn't truncate, (7) DEBUG level never truncates. Test `formatLogArgs`: (1) returns array with single formatted string. Test `formatTimestamp`: (1) returns [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] with brackets, (2) zero-pads single digits.

subtasks.title: Test: formatLogArgs returns single string array

tasks.title: Write formatter.ts color and utility function tests
tasks.description: Add to `packages/logger/test/formatter.test.ts`. Test `getColorFunction`: (1) returns a function for each LoggerColors value, (2) function returns a string. Test `getCategoryBackgroundFunction(color)`: (1) returns chalk function with background, (2) text uses contrasting color. Test `shouldUseWhiteText`: (1) returns true for dark colors: BLACK, RED, BLUE, MAGENTA, (2) returns false for light colors: GREEN, YELLOW, CYAN, WHITE, GRAY + bright variants. Test smoke: `levelIcons` has icon for all levels, `levelColors` has color for all levels, `levelBgColors` has bg color for all levels.

tasks.title: Write redactSensitiveData isolated tests
tasks.description: Add to `packages/logger/test/formatter.test.ts`. Test `redactSensitiveData` function directly (now exported). Scenarios: (1) key='password' + value='secret123' -> '[REDACTED]', (2) key='name' + value='John' -> 'John' (no redaction), (3) string containing JWT -> '[REDACTED]', (4) array of objects -> recursive redaction, (5) nested object -> redacts keys at all levels, (6) case-insensitive: key='PASSWORD' + value='secret' -> '[REDACTED]', (7) non-string non-object values pass through unchanged.

tasks.title: Write categories.ts tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/logger/test/categories.test.ts`. Scenarios: (1) registerCategoryInternal registers a new category, (2) getCategoryByKey returns DEFAULT for unknown keys, (3) getCategoryByKey returns correct category for registered keys, (4) getMaxCategoryNameLength returns length of longest registered category name, (5) clearCategories clears all except DEFAULT. Import from `../src/categories` (or index). Use beforeEach to clearCategories for isolation.

tasks.title: Write config.ts tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/logger/test/config.test.ts`. Scenarios: (1) configureLogger updates config with partial merge, (2) getConfig returns current config, (3) resetLoggerConfig restores defaults, (4) partial update preserves unmodified fields. Use beforeEach to resetLoggerConfig for isolation.

tasks.title: Write environment.ts tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/logger/test/environment.test.ts`. Use vi.stubEnv for env vars. Scenarios: getBooleanEnv: (1) null if var not set, (2) true for 'true', (3) false for 'false'/'0'/'yes'. getNumberEnv: (4) parsed int, (5) null if NaN, (6) null if not set. getLogLevelEnv: (7) valid LogLevel, (8) null for invalid. getConfigFromEnv: (9) without categoryKey uses LOG_ prefix, (10) with categoryKey uses LOG_{CATEGORY}_ prefix with LOG_ fallback, (11) returns Partial<BaseLoggerConfig>, (12) reads LOG_LEVEL, LOG_SAVE, LOG_TRUNCATE_LONG_TEXT as correct types, (13) returns defaults when vars undefined. IMPORTANT: NO LOG_FORMAT variable exists.

tasks.title: Write createEmailClient tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/email/test/client.test.ts`. Scenarios: (1) createEmailClient({apiKey: 'test-key'}) returns instance with emails.send available, (2) createEmailClient without apiKey and without RESEND_API_KEY env var throws 'Missing API key...' error. Use vi.stubEnv to control RESEND_API_KEY. Note: Resend constructor does NOT validate key against server (only on first request).

tasks.title: Write BaseLayout template tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/email/test/templates/base-layout.test.tsx`. Use `import { render } from '@react-email/render'` (ASYNC). Scenarios: (1) renders children correctly (content appears in HTML), (2) with showUnsubscribe=true (default) includes 'Si no deseas recibir estos correos, puedes' + 'darte de baja aquí' with link to https://hospeda.com.ar/unsubscribe, (3) with showUnsubscribe=false does NOT include unsubscribe text, (4) header contains 'Hospeda', (5) footer contains 'Hospeda - Alojamientos turísticos en Concepción del Uruguay y el Litoral'. ALL assertions in SPANISH.

tasks.title: Write VerifyEmailTemplate tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/email/test/templates/verify-email.test.tsx`. Use `await render(<VerifyEmailTemplate ... />)`. Props: {name: string, verificationUrl: string}. Scenarios: (1) renders 'Hola {name},' with user name, (2) heading 'Verifica tu dirección de correo electrónico', (3) text 'Gracias por registrarte en Hospeda...', (4) text 'Haz clic en el botón de abajo...', (5) button 'Verificar correo electrónico' with verificationUrl as href, (6) expiration 'Este enlace expira en 24 horas', (7) security note 'Si no solicitaste esta verificación...', (8) fallback text 'O copia y pega este enlace en tu navegador:', (9) uses BaseLayout (HTML contains Hospeda structure), (10) name='' doesn't crash, renders 'Hola ,'. ALL text in SPANISH.

tasks.title: Write ResetPasswordTemplate tests
tasks.description: Create `packages/email/test/templates/reset-password.test.tsx`. Use `await render(<ResetPasswordTemplate ... />)`. Props: {name: string, resetUrl: string} (resetUrl NOT resetPasswordUrl). Scenarios: (1) renders 'Hola {name},' with name, (2) heading 'Restablece tu contraseña', (3) text 'Recibimos una solicitud para restablecer...', (4) button 'Restablecer contraseña' with resetUrl as href, (5) expiration 'Este enlace expira en 1 hora por motivos de seguridad...', (6) bold '¿No solicitaste esto?' + security text, (7) fallback 'O copia y pega este enlace en tu navegador:', (8) uses BaseLayout, (9) name='' doesn't crash. ALL text in SPANISH.

tasks.title: Update auth-ui vitest.config.ts coverage thresholds to 90%
tasks.description: Edit `packages/auth-ui/vitest.config.ts` and change coverage thresholds from 70/70/60/70 to 90/90/90/90 (lines, functions, branches, statements). Run coverage to verify it passes: `cd packages/auth-ui && pnpm vitest run --coverage`.

tasks.title: Update billing vitest.config.ts coverage thresholds to 90%
tasks.description: Edit `packages/billing/vitest.config.ts` and change coverage thresholds from 70/70/60/70 to 90/90/90/90. Run coverage: `cd packages/billing && pnpm vitest run --coverage`.

tasks.title: Update logger vitest.config.ts coverage thresholds to 90%
tasks.description: Edit `packages/logger/vitest.config.ts` and change coverage thresholds from 70/70/60/70 to 90/90/90/90. IMPORTANT: Ensure the coverage exclude pattern filters out the compiled .js/.d.ts/.js.map/.d.ts.map files in src/. Run coverage: `cd packages/logger && pnpm vitest run --coverage`.

subtasks.title: Add exclude for compiled files if needed

tasks.title: Verify email package coverage thresholds at 90%
tasks.description: The vitest.config.ts created in T-004 already has 90% thresholds. Run coverage: `cd packages/email && pnpm vitest run --coverage`. Verify all 4 metrics (lines, functions, branches, statements) are >=90%.

tasks.title: Run full quality gate on auth-ui package
tasks.description: Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` on auth-ui. Run `pnpm test` for all auth-ui tests. Verify: (1) 0 type errors, (2) 0 lint errors (biome), (3) all tests pass, (4) no .only() or hardcoded .skip(), (5) all test files use AAA pattern, (6) no `as any` without justification, (7) no URLSearchParams mock for ResetPasswordForm/VerifyEmail.

tasks.title: Run full quality gate on billing package
tasks.description: Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` on billing. Run `pnpm test` for all billing tests. Verify: (1) 0 type errors, (2) 0 lint errors, (3) all tests pass, (4) imports use .js extension, (5) no .only/.skip.

tasks.title: Run full quality gate on logger package
tasks.description: Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` on logger. Run `pnpm test` for all logger tests. Verify: (1) 0 type errors, (2) 0 lint errors, (3) all tests pass, (4) no test mocks @repo/logger inside logger's own tests, (5) no .only/.skip.

tasks.title: Run full quality gate on email package
tasks.description: Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` on email. Run `pnpm test` for all email tests. Verify: (1) 0 type errors, (2) 0 lint errors, (3) all tests pass, (4) templates assert Spanish text, (5) sendEmail tests never use .toThrow(), (6) no .only/.skip.

tasks.title: Run global pnpm test and verify all 4 packages pass together
tasks.description: Run `pnpm test` from monorepo root. Verify all 4 packages (auth-ui, billing, logger, email) pass without errors. Check that no cross-package test pollution exists. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` from root. Update SPEC-040 status from 'draft' to 'completed'.
